headlines.title: Energy Security, Not Climate Goals, Is Now Driving the Clean Power Boom
headlines.summary: While ships are beginning to trickle through the Strait of Hormuz after months of near-total closure, it will be a very long time before the effects of this year’s energy crisis fade from the global economy – if they ever do. headlines.title: Iraq Is Keeping Its Syria Oil Route—Even If Hormuz Reopens
headlines.summary: Nobody, especially not Iraq, wants to be caught relying on Hormuz ever again. headlines.summary: The Middle East's latest peace deal just got a peace deal of its own. headlines.title: Russia Is Rationing Fuel in Moscow After Drone Strikes

headlines.title: Kuwait Says Oil Output Could Hit 2 Million Bpd Within a Week
headlines.summary: Kuwait expects to raise its oil production to 2 million barrels per day (bpd) within a week, up from an average of 573,000 bpd in May, amid the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
